problem_statement: find_by_trigger() uses keyword set-intersection. Turn 001's MMD report documents 5 false positives from 3 innocuous code shapes. These false positives degrade gate credibility and create waiver busywork. Replace with TF-IDF cosine similarity computed at registry load time. Same interface, better signal.

prior_solutions_considered.rejected_because: Requires network call at match time. Hidden constraint: no internet access at match time. Also introduces an external API dependency for a library operation.
Model download required on first use (~90MB). Overkill for a 20-sentence corpus. TF-IDF captures the vocabulary overlap that matters here without the weight.

prior_solutions_considered.approach: Retain keyword matching with a stop-word list
prior_solutions_considered.rejected_because: Would require maintaining a custom stop-word list and re-testing on every new card. The false positive problem is structural, not a list problem. Cosine similarity handles it correctly.

claims.claim: TF-IDF cosine similarity will score the turn 001 false positives (e.g. 'reads structured data and produces formatted file output' vs DLQ_ACCUMULATION triggers) below a reasonable threshold
TF-IDF cosine similarity will score the true positives (e.g. 'retry loop around external API call' vs RETRY_AMPLIFICATION triggers) above that same threshold
scikit-learn TfidfVectorizer and cosine_similarity are sufficient — no GPU, no model download, sub-millisecond at this corpus size
The existing find_by_trigger() interface (takes a string, returns a ranked List[PatternCard]) can be preserved exactly while swapping the implementation

known_constraints: Same function signature — find_by_trigger(code_description: str) -> List[PatternCard]
TF-IDF vectors built from trigger_shapes corpus at PatternRegistry.__init__ time
No network calls at match time
Threshold must be calibrated against turn 001 false positives before shipping
scikit-learn is the only new dependency
